378	\subsection{The genus-$0$ arm of the witness existence}
379	\label{sec:genusZeroWitness}
380	
381	The genus-$0$ sub-case of \cref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness} admits an explicit witness construction at the Lean level, packaged separately from the general existence statement so that the genus-$0$ branch can be discharged independently once the shared cotangent-vanishing pile lands (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile} pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii)).
382	
383	\begin{theorem}[Genus-$0$ Albanese witness]
384	  \label{def:genusZeroWitness}
385	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness}
386	  \uses{def:JacobianWitness, thm:rigidity_over_kbar, def:genus}
387	  \notready
388	  Let $C \in \mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ be a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over $k$ with $\genus(C) = 0$. Then there exists a $\mathtt{JacobianWitness}\,C$ whose underlying scheme is $\Spec k$ (the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$), with the trivial group-object structure, smoothness of relative dimension $0$, properness, and geometric irreducibility, and whose $\mathtt{isAlbaneseFor}$ field encodes the Albanese universal property for every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$.
389	\end{theorem}
390	
391	\begin{proof}
392	  \uses{thm:rigidity_over_kbar, thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen, def:IsAlbanese, def:JacobianWitness, def:genus}
393	  \leanok
394	  We construct the seven fields of \cref{def:JacobianWitness}.
395	
396	  \paragraph{Underlying scheme.} Take $J := \mathbf{1}$, the terminal object of $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, which on underlying schemes is $\Spec k$ viewed as a $k$-scheme via the identity structure morphism.
397	
398	  \paragraph{Group-object structure, properness, smoothness, geometric irreducibility.} The terminal object $\mathbf{1}$ of any cartesian-monoidal category carries the (unique) trivial group-object structure: multiplication and inverse are the unique morphisms to $\mathbf{1}$, and the identity element is the identity morphism. The structure morphism $\mathbf{1}.\mathtt{hom} = \mathrm{id}_{\Spec k}$ is proper (the identity is a closed immersion, in particular proper), smooth (the identity is unramified and flat, hence smooth), and geometrically irreducible. The geometric-irreducibility instance is the project-local helper \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.geometricallyIrreducible\_id\_Spec} (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:120}--\texttt{126}), which derives geometric irreducibility of the identity on $\Spec k$ from the fact that, on the geometric fibre $\Spec K$ over any field extension $k \to K$, the second projection of the pullback square is an isomorphism, and $\Spec K$ is irreducible.
399	
400	  \paragraph{Smoothness of relative dimension $\genus\,C$.} The smoothness witness $\mathtt{smoothGenus}$ is required at relative dimension $\genus\,C$. The hypothesis $\genus\,C = 0$ rewrites the target relative dimension to $0$, after which the goal reduces to $\mathtt{SmoothOfRelativeDimension}\ 0\ (\mathrm{id}_{\Spec k})$. This is a Mathlib instance: the identity morphism on any base is smooth of relative dimension $0$.
401	
402	  \paragraph{The \texttt{isAlbaneseFor} field.} For every $k$-rational marked point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$, we exhibit an \cref{def:IsAlbanese} term for the triple $(C, P, J)$.
403	
404	  The universal pointed morphism is the unique terminal map $\alpha := \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \colon C \to \mathbf{1}$ (the structure morphism of $C$ as a $k$-scheme, factored through the terminal object). The pointed condition $P \circ \alpha = \eta_J = \mathrm{id}_{\mathbf{1}}$ holds automatically: both sides are morphisms $\mathbf{1} \to \mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$, and the terminal object has a unique endomorphism.
405	
406	  For the universal factorisation: given a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme $A$ over $k$ with identity element $\eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$ and a morphism $f \colon C \to A$ with $P \circ f = \eta_A$, we must exhibit a unique morphism $g \colon J \to A$ such that $f = \alpha \circ g$. \emph{Existence}: take $g := \eta_A \colon \mathbf{1} \to A$. Then $\alpha \circ g = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$, which equals $f$ precisely when $f$ is the constant morphism at $\eta_A$. This is the content of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (the project's named rigidity declaration of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar}, established directly over the base field $k$ per the iter-127 over-k commitment): the supplied marked point $P \in C(k)$ feeds the pointing hypothesis, and the rigidity conclusion is exactly $f = \mathtt{toUnit}\,C \circ \eta_A$ as scheme morphisms. \emph{Uniqueness}: any two morphisms $\mathbf{1} \to A$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ agreeing on $P \circ (-) = \eta_A$ are equal by the universal property of the terminal object $\mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ as a category (a morphism out of $\mathbf{1}$ in $\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec k)$ is determined by where it sends the unique generator, here pinned to $\eta_A$).
407	
408	  \paragraph{Vacuity for the $C(k) = \emptyset$ branch.} When no $k$-rational point $P : \mathbf{1} \to C$ exists (Brauer--Severi conics over $\mathbb Q$ are the standard counterexample), the universal quantifier $\forall (P : \mathbf{1} \to C), \mathtt{IsAlbanese}\,C\,P\,J$ is vacuously satisfied at the Lean type level: Lean's $\forall$ over an empty type is trivially true. The construction handles this branch implicitly without any explicit case split.
409	
410	  \paragraph{Body closure status.} The Lean body of \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness} (\texttt{AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:174}--\texttt{178}) is currently \texttt{sorry}; the closure is gated on the body of \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} (M2.a), which in turn depends on pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) of the shared cotangent-vanishing pile (\cref{sec:RigidityKbar_shared_pile}) landing in iter-129+. Earliest realistic body-closure iteration: iter-138+.
411	\end{proof}
412	
413	\section{Implementation route via the Albanese functor}
414	
415	Because the full Picard-scheme representability theorem is not available in current Mathlib, the formal construction proceeds in two layers:
416	
417	\begin{enumerate}
418	  \item \textbf{Layer I --- direct definition.} The Jacobian of $C$ is defined uniformly as the underlying scheme of the Albanese witness provided by Theorem~\ref{thm:nonempty_jacobianWitness}, carrying the four properties of Theorems~\ref{thm:Jacobian_grpObj}--\ref{thm:Jacobian_geomIrred} (each obtained by projection from the witness). The genus-$0$ case is included: the witness's universal property in that case carries the rigidity content for morphisms from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible genus-$0$ curve to a smooth proper geometrically irreducible group scheme. Per the iter-127 over-k commitment (\texttt{analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md}), this rigidity is established directly over the base field $k$ via the named declaration \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar} of \cref{chap:RigidityKbar} and the shared cotangent-vanishing pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii); no base-change to $\bar k$ and no Galois descent of morphism equality enters. The genus-$0$ arm is packaged as the explicit Lean construction \cref{def:genusZeroWitness}: the underlying scheme is the terminal object $\Spec k$, the universal morphism in the $C(k) \neq \emptyset$ branch is the unique terminal map \texttt{toUnit}\,$C$, and the universal factorisation reduces to \cref{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}.
419	
420	  \item \textbf{Layer II --- Abel--Jacobi compatibility.} Chapter~\ref{chap:AbelJacobi} introduces the Abel--Jacobi morphism $C \to \Jac(C)$ and shows that it is universal among morphisms from $C$ to abelian varieties.
421	\end{enumerate}
422

---

# Session 127 — Iter-127 review summary

## Metadata

- **Iteration**: 127
- **Session**: session_127
- **Stage**: prover (third consecutive plan-phase-only iter)
- **Model**: opus
- **Sorry count entering iter-127**: 2
- **Sorry count leaving iter-127**: 3 (+1 from M2.b scaffold)
- **Per-file distribution at close**:
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:178` — `genusZeroWitness` (NEW iter-127 scaffold)
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/Jacobian.lean:197` — `nonempty_jacobianWitness` (off-limits, unchanged)
  - `AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityKbar.lean:87` — `rigidity_over_kbar` (iter-126 scaffold, unchanged)

## Targets attempted

**None.** Iter-127 was plan-phase-only by design (`meta.json planValidate.status: ok_intentional_skip / objectives: 0`; `prover.durationSecs: 0`). The prover stage was intentionally skipped per the iter-126 commitment; the iter-127 plan-agent landed structural work via subagent dispatches instead:

1. **Refactor `refactor-m2b-scaffold-iter127`** (Option A) → COMPLETE. Added `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness` declaration at `Jacobian.lean:174–178` with a single `sorry` body. +1 project sorry; no cascading breakage; compiles clean; `lean_verify` returns kernel + `sorryAx` only.
2. **Mathlib-analogist `cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k-iter127`** → OK_OVER_K on all 3 pile pieces. Persistent file `analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md` written. **Strategic adoption**: M2.c (Galois descent of morphism equality, ~300–500 LOC / 4–8 iter) + M2.c.aux (`geomIrred.exists_kalg_pt` PHANTOM) DROPPED iter-127. Net savings: 7–13 iter / 500–900 LOC. Honest M2 closure estimate revised iter-150+ → iter-143+.
3. **Blueprint-writer `rigiditykbar-piece-i-iter127`** → COMPLETE. +101 lines to `RigidityKbar.tex`; 5 lemma blocks (`lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra`, `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, `lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises`, `lem:GrpObj_omega_free`, `lem:GrpObj_omega_rank_eq_dim`) + 1 remark for piece (i) sub-decomposition. Stages the iter-128 META-PATTERN TRIPWIRE prover lane on `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` → `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra`.
4. **Blueprint-writer `jacobian-iter127`** → COMPLETE. +31 lines to `Jacobian.tex`; `def:genusZeroWitness` block + proof sketch + C.2.f-DROPPED + C.2.g over-k inventory + route-γ rewrite + Layer-I update + `\uses{thm:rigidity_over_kbar}` cross-ref added.
5. **3 mandatory plan-phase critics**:
   - `strategy-critic-iter127` → CHALLENGE (5 CHALLENGE + 2 critical alternative + 2 SOUND). All 5 CHALLENGEs + 2 alternatives addressed inline via STRATEGY.md edits.
   - `blueprint-reviewer-iter127` → PARTIAL (3 must-fix). 2 resolved by wave-2 writer dispatches; 1 resolved by the over-k analogist verdict.
   - `progress-critic-iter127` → 1 CHURNING (META-PATTERN — 3 consecutive plan-phase-only iters) + 2 UNCLEAR (fresh M2.a, M2.b routes). META-PATTERN corrective STAGED this iter via piece-(i) writer dispatch; iter-128 must enact (prover dispatch on `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra`).

## Per-target detail

### `AlgebraicGeometry.genusZeroWitness` (Jacobian.lean:178) — NEW iter-127 scaffold

- **Status**: not_started (scaffold; +1 project sorry).
- **Source of edit**: `refactor-m2b-scaffold-iter127` (NOT a prover attempt).
- **Signature** (verbatim):
  ```lean
  noncomputable def genusZeroWitness (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
      [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible C.hom]
      (h : genus C = 0) :
      JacobianWitness C :=
    sorry
  ```
- **Blueprint anchor**: `Jacobian.tex` § "The genus-0 arm of the witness existence" (added iter-127, +31 lines). `def:genusZeroWitness` block has `\uses{def:JacobianWitness, thm:rigidity_over_kbar, def:genus}` + 6-paragraph proof sketch covering all 7 fields (J, grpObj, proper, smooth, geomIrred, smoothGenus, isAlbaneseFor) + vacuity branch for `C(k) = ∅`.
- **Body-closure ETA**: iter-146+ (after M2.a body closes iter-144+).
- **Off-limits this iter**: yes (no prover lane).

### `AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_over_kbar` (RigidityKbar.lean:87) — unchanged iter-126 scaffold

- **Status**: not_started; unchanged at the Lean level this iter.
- **Blueprint extension this iter**: +101 lines to `RigidityKbar.tex` (5 lemma blocks for piece (i) sub-decomposition + 1 remark; iter-128 first prover target staged as `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra`).
- **Strategic change this iter**: over-k commitment via the iter-127 analogist verdict — the chapter introduction was rewritten by the plan-agent to drop the over-`k̄` + base-change-and-Galois-descent framing; M2.c and M2.c.aux DROPPED from the critical path.
- **Body-closure ETA**: iter-144+ (after pile pieces (i)+(ii)+(iii) land iter-128 through iter-143).

### `AlgebraicGeometry.nonempty_jacobianWitness` (Jacobian.lean:197) — off-limits

- **Status**: not_started; unchanged this iter.
- **Blueprint extension this iter**: indirect — the new `def:genusZeroWitness` block sets up the genus-0 arm that will be consumed by `nonempty_jacobianWitness`'s iter-148+ genus-stratified restructure.
- **Off-limits**: yes (Phase C/E packaging; queued behind M2.a + M2.b body closures).

## Key findings

- **Over-k commitment is a load-bearing iter-127 result.** The mathlib-analogist verdict (`OK_OVER_K` on all 3 active pile pieces — cotangent triviality via functorial shear iso, `Differential.ContainConstants` k-agnostic, char-`p` via **absolute** Frobenius) eliminates M2.c + M2.c.aux from the critical path and revises the honest M2-closure estimate by ~7 iters. The persistent file `analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md` documents the decision rationale.
- **META-PATTERN TRIPWIRE is now staged, not deferred.** The iter-127 progress-critic flagged 3 consecutive plan-phase-only iters as CHURNING; the iter-128 prover lane on `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` is concretely prepared (Lean target name pinned by blueprint-writer; sub-lemma decomposition + ETA in `RigidityKbar.tex`). If iter-128 is also plan-phase-only, the meta-pattern flips to STUCK + user escalation.
- **No new axioms.** `archon-protected.yaml` unchanged (9 protected declarations at original paths with unchanged signatures). The iter-127 scaffold `genusZeroWitness` is a non-protected leaf declaration; `lean_verify` returns `propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound, sorryAx` only (refactor agent report).

## CRITICAL

1. **iter-128 META-PATTERN TRIPWIRE MUST fire.** Per the iter-127 progress-critic, 3 consecutive plan-phase-only iters (iter-125/126/127) trigger the CHURNING meta-pattern. If iter-128 is also plan-phase-only, the verdict flips to STUCK + user escalation (4 consecutive plan-phase-only iters). The iter-127 plan-agent has staged the corrective: the first piece-(i) sub-lemma `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` → `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra` is now blueprint-ready with proof sketch + `\lean{...}` hint + signature in `RigidityKbar.tex`. Iter-128 plan-phase MUST:
   - (a) dispatch a refactor to scaffold the Lean declaration `AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra` in a new file (likely `AlgebraicJacobian/Cotangent/GrpObj.lean`, or expand the existing `Rigidity.lean`/`RigidityKbar.lean` if the agent prefers single-file consolidation);
   - (b) dispatch a prover lane on the same declaration in the same iter (NOT defer to iter-129).
   - Optional add-on if time allows: dispatch the `rigidity_over_kbar` → `rigidity_over_k` rename refactor (low-risk cleanup matching the iter-127 over-k commitment).

2. **iter-128 prover target options** (iter-127 plan-agent suggested two; pick ONE for the iter-128 first lane):
   - `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra` (the Lie algebra definition itself — cotangent at identity packaged as a finitely-generated free `k`-module). Natural starter if the prover prefers building from scratch.
   - `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank` (the rank-equals-relative-dimension claim about an already-existing finite-free module). Smaller signature; may be easier as a first prover round.

## HIGH

3. **No over-`k̄` regression.** The iter-127 over-k commitment is load-bearing for the M2-closure ETA revision (iter-150+ → iter-143+; 7-13 iter savings). Any iter-128+ refactor or strategy critic that proposes reintroducing the over-`k̄` framing or M2.c (Galois descent) MUST be cross-referenced against `analogies/cotangent-vanishing-pile-over-k.md` and (if needed) re-litigated via a fresh mathlib-analogist consult before adoption. The over-k path is now load-bearing.

4. **Reconcile `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-rigiditykbar-iter127` informational findings** (will arrive in `.archon/task_results/`). The piece-(i) lemma blocks added to `RigidityKbar.tex` reference Lean targets that don't yet exist (`AlgebraicGeometry.GrpObj.lieAlgebra` etc.); the checker will flag these as missing `\lean{...}` resolutions. This is expected; the iter-128 plan-agent should consume these as the prover target list rather than as a must-fix-this-iter blocker.

## MEDIUM

5. **Watch: shear-iso formulation risk.** The iter-127 over-k analogist's risk register explicitly mandates the **functorial** shear-iso globalisation for piece (i.b) — NOT pointwise translation via `k̄`-rational points. If the iter-128 prover lane on (i.b) discovers the shear-iso formulation isn't viable in Mathlib `b80f227`'s current `GrpObj` API (e.g. some required `mul_assoc` lemma is missing on the relevant `Over` category), the project may need to fall back to the over-`k̄` baseline + reintroduce M2.c. Cost: ~1 iter strategic backtrack.

6. **Iter-129+ kickoff staging.** With piece-(i.a) closed iter-128, iter-129 should fire the next sub-lemma in the (i) chain (`lem:GrpObj_mulRight_globalises` or `lem:GrpObj_lieAlgebra_finrank`, whichever didn't go in iter-128). The pile build proceeds iter-by-iter through (i.b) → (i.c) → (ii) → (iii) → M2.a body (iter-144+) → M2.b body (iter-146+) → `nonempty_jacobianWitness` genus-stratified restructure (iter-148+).

## LOW
